economic_notes: Nation holds investment in 1252729 B.C. Ltd. (incorporated June 2020) which purchased fee simple property in BC. Contingent payments of $1,800,000 expected from Trans Mountain Pipeline LP ($900,000) and Vancouver Fraser Port Authority ($900,000). Nation is part of Quw'utsun Nation group (Cowichan Tribes, Halalt First Nation, Penelakut Tribe, Lyackson First Nation, Stz'uminus First Nation). Nation manages $910,000 Fortis BC funding agreement on behalf of Quw'utsun Nation.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Nation has a subsidiary 1252729 B.C. Ltd. with negative shareholders equity of -$763, held at cost. Deferred revenue of $1,891,450 is primarily unspent BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing funds ($1,861,450). Nation acts as flow-through manager for $910,000 Fortis BC funding agreement on behalf of Quw'utsun Nation members. Contingent assets of $1,800,000 from Trans Mountain and Vancouver Fraser Port Authority not recognized on balance sheet. Total government transfers calculated as sum of ISC ($385,177), DFO ($102,524), Province of BC ($204,487), First Nations Health Authority ($17,000), Children and Family Development ($30,000), Parks Canada ($133,523), Coast Salish Employment & Training Society ($98,274), First Nation Education Steering Committee ($7,286), George Massey Crossing ($10,000), H'ulh'etun Health Society ($1,000), Hul'qumi'num Treaty Group ($239,950), Specific Claims ($163,026), BC First Nation Gaming Revenue Sharing ($78,951), BC Special ($487), Transmountain Consultations ($10,000), Port of Vancouver ($19,000), Delta Port 4 ($217,140), General Referrals ($43,182), Transmountain Accommodation ($874,780) and Fortis BC Tilbury LNG ($56,100). Remuneration schedules exist but amounts not included in extracted text. expenses_education includes tuition/books ($164,246) and education recognition ($12,445). No long-term debt identified.
